title: Congressional Ball

jokes.text: How did you know I have a minivan? - Just a good guess.
jokes.setup: Selina offers to let Nickerson keep mugs for her minivan
jokes.punchline: Selina's 'good guess' reveals her stereotypical assumptions about suburban congresswomen
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment showing Selina's condescending profiling disguised as charm

jokes.text: Ugh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Nickerson enthusiastically describes being a stay-at-home mother of six
jokes.punchline: Selina's involuntary disgusted reaction to traditional motherhood
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat - Selina can't hide her revulsion despite political necessity

jokes.text: I mean, my license expired a few years ago, but I get the general idea.
jokes.setup: Pretending to relate to Nickerson about minivan driving
jokes.punchline: Reveals she doesn't even have a valid driver's license
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal of how disconnected Selina is from normal life while trying to fake relatability

jokes.text: My Donald. He has chemo today. (GASPS) Fantastic.
jokes.setup: Nickerson mentions her husband has chemotherapy today
jokes.punchline: Selina enthusiastically responds 'Fantastic' to cancer treatment
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Selina's political autopilot leads to horrifically inappropriate response

jokes.text: Check out the tits in the third row. I could dribble those things like basketballs.
jokes.setup: Jonah at a campaign rally
jokes.punchline: Makes crude sexual comments while mic is presumably hot
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate crudeness but relies on shock value rather than cleverness

jokes.text: Drop the puck? - Common hockey term.
jokes.setup: Mike uses 'drop the puck' as military metaphor
jokes.punchline: Doubles down on the nonsensical sports metaphor when questioned
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mike's desperate attempt to sound authoritative

jokes.text: Oh, I just heard the buzzer. That is the end of the third period. See you at the next face-off.
jokes.setup: Mike's hockey metaphors continue inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Commits fully to the sports metaphor to escape difficult questions
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the hockey metaphor callback, perfectly executed escape mechanism

jokes.text: Well, I suppose putting a few pictures up of Muhammad never hurt anyone.
jokes.setup: Discussion about celebrating various religious holidays
jokes.punchline: Oblivious reference to Charlie Hebdo-style controversies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy showing Selina's complete political tone-deafness on sensitive issues

jokes.text: She's a lesbian, Mike. She's not a werewolf. Although either one would explain why she never shaves her legs.
jokes.setup: Mike being overly delicate about Catherine's 'condition'
jokes.punchline: Selina's casual homophobia escalates to personal insult about her daughter
jokes.explanation: Perfect three-part escalation showing Selina's complete lack of maternal warmth

jokes.text: When does the White House's most useless press secretary list come out? I can't wait to see who's number one this year. - I voted for Mike.
jokes.setup: Selina sarcastically asks about useless press secretary list
jokes.punchline: Ben matter-of-factly says he voted for Mike
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery from Ben, perfect character consistency

jokes.text: I don't think you're in it this year, Ame.
jokes.setup: Amy complaining about unwanted attention from hot list
jokes.punchline: Dan casually reveals she's not on it anymore
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Dan's casual cruelty disguised as helpfulness

jokes.text: There you go, Amy. Problem solved.
jokes.setup: Amy's embarrassment about not being on the hot list
jokes.punchline: Dan's tone-deaf 'solution'
jokes.explanation: Dan's characteristically oblivious follow-up compounds Amy's humiliation

jokes.text: Who's 22? The Elephant Man?
jokes.setup: Gary shocked at his ranking on hot list
jokes.punchline: Incredulous comparison to famous disfigured person
jokes.explanation: Moderately funny self-deprecation but relies on dated reference

jokes.text: This year? It's the 50 Hottest Staffers, Jonah, not the 50 people most likely to kill themselves before trial.
jokes.setup: Jonah complaining about not being on hot list
jokes.punchline: Dan's brutal comparison to suicide risk list
jokes.explanation: Exceptional insult with perfect structure and character voice - becomes quotable reference

jokes.text: I am on a career rocket ship to Mars right now, Dan, except I'm gonna leave Matt Damon there because the guy made potatoes in his own shit like a fucking animal.
jokes.setup: Jonah defending his career trajectory
jokes.punchline: Bizarre Martian reference with scatological details
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah rambling metaphor with gross-out humor, character-consistent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Find a woman who was on it last year, but not this year. I mean, that's like a 'make her pay for dinner' situation.
jokes.setup: Dan explaining how to use the hot list strategically
jokes.punchline: Explicitly misogynistic dating strategy based on perceived decline
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment perfectly capturing Dan's calculating cruelty

jokes.text: If you don't look like someone melted Play-Doh all over a flagpole, it does.
jokes.setup: Question about whether Dan's strategy works
jokes.punchline: Vivid description of Jonah's appearance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor for Jonah's appearance, highly quotable and original

jokes.text: Well, it makes sense. He's got beautiful eyes.
jokes.setup: Outrage about Gary being ranked #21 on hot list
jokes.punchline: Selina's matter-of-fact defense of Gary's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment showing Selina's odd protective relationship with Gary

jokes.text: And once you're all done with them, I'll move in and squirt a half gallon of ropey jism into their modeled congressional cornholes.
jokes.setup: Roger explaining the vote-getting strategy professionally
jokes.punchline: Extremely graphic sexual metaphor for political persuasion
jokes.explanation: Peak Roger character moment - professional tone with shocking vulgarity

jokes.text: Oh, you made it so far and then you just... (SIGHS)
jokes.setup: Roger's surprisingly professional briefing
jokes.punchline: Selina's disappointed reaction to his inevitable crude finish
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat showing familiarity with Roger's pattern

jokes.text: No, Roger took my thing about ropey jism.
jokes.setup: Asked if he has anything to add to the briefing
jokes.punchline: Tom claims ownership of the crude sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback revealing Tom's surprising vulgarity beneath polished exterior

jokes.text: Literally anything.
jokes.setup: Question about what O'Brien could offer Tom better than VP
jokes.punchline: Ben's blunt assessment of VP value
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben delivery - maximum cynicism with minimal words

jokes.text: Well, as the late, great Lionel Richie once said, 'Oh, what a feeling, I am dancing on the debt ceiling.'
jokes.setup: Question about debt ceiling vote
jokes.punchline: Garbled song lyrics with wrong artist attribution
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah malapropism showing his confident ignorance

jokes.text: OKAY, A: Lionel Richie is not dead, AND B: what the fuck does that even mean?
jokes.setup: Jonah's mangled quote attribution
jokes.punchline: Dan's systematic deconstruction of everything wrong
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response highlighting multiple layers of Jonah's stupidity

jokes.text: you sentient enema
jokes.setup: Dan arguing with Jonah about campaign success
jokes.punchline: Creative medical insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant original insult - 'sentient enema' is both creative and perfectly descriptive

jokes.text: Mother Mary dry humping a pillow. That is what I call natural talent.
jokes.setup: Judy Sherman's competent political answer
jokes.punchline: Dan's crude religious expletive of admiration
jokes.explanation: Inventive profanity showing Dan's admiration for political skill

jokes.text: Feel my dick. I am rock-hard for you right now, mister. You think I'm kidding. Feel it.
jokes.setup: Dan excited about Richard's performance as Jonah
jokes.punchline: Extremely inappropriate sexual excitement in professional context
jokes.explanation: Peak inappropriate Dan moment, character-consistent but relies on shock value

jokes.text: Granted, every town up here is just two dirty piles of snow connected by a covered bridge
jokes.setup: Dan criticizing Jonah's geographic ignorance
jokes.punchline: Dismissive description of New England towns
jokes.explanation: Vivid, condescending description perfectly capturing Dan's urban elitism

jokes.text: I don't know. Maybe I was supposed to learn it in the second grade, but my teacher was a fucking bitch.
jokes.setup: Jonah explaining his geographic ignorance
jokes.punchline: Blames childhood teacher with unnecessary profanity
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate deflection showing Jonah's inability to accept responsibility

jokes.text: If you were like 10% less black, I could make you president.
jokes.setup: Dan talking strategy with Richard
jokes.punchline: Casually racist political calculation
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment showing Dan's calculating view of demographics

jokes.text: Does she know I'm not the president of Cuba?
jokes.setup: Selina looking at decorations designed by congressman's wife
jokes.punchline: Assumes the tropical theme indicates confusion about her office
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's narcissistic assumptions about others' competence

jokes.text: Are you wearing cologne? A little. (SNIFFS) Smells like birdseed.
jokes.setup: Selina notices Gary is wearing cologne
jokes.punchline: Her assessment that it smells like birdseed
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing their comfortable insult dynamic

jokes.text: Do you want to play it more like a mystery?
jokes.setup: Discussion about handling Catherine's sexuality publicly
jokes.punchline: Selina suggests treating it like a game show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - treating personal issues as political strategy

jokes.text: Okay, can you just speak English, Catherine? 'Cause I don't know what you're trying to say.
jokes.setup: Catherine saying she wants to keep things 'on the down-low'
jokes.punchline: Selina's complete inability to understand basic slang
jokes.explanation: Good generational comedy showing Selina's disconnect from youth culture

jokes.text: We'll keep it under the gaydar.
jokes.setup: Agreeing to keep Catherine's relationship private
jokes.punchline: Mangled 'radar/gaydar' pun
jokes.explanation: Moderate pun showing Selina's attempt at being hip falling flat

jokes.text: Who doesn't love a cappella, right? It's like a magic trick 'cause there are no instruments, of course.
jokes.setup: A cappella group performing
jokes.punchline: Selina's condescending over-explanation of obvious concept
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's patronizing tendency to explain obvious things

jokes.text: You mean 11:00? Over there? Seriously, can you really not tell time?
jokes.setup: Gary indicating Tom's location as '8:00'
jokes.punchline: Selina's incredulous reaction to clock position confusion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Selina's impatience with Gary's basic incompetence

jokes.text: The fluffers have done their work. It's time for the orgy to begin.
jokes.setup: Roger reporting that prep work is done for vote gathering
jokes.punchline: Pornography metaphor for political persuasion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roger character moment - professional tone with crude metaphor

jokes.text: That's not his wife. - Okay. - This is one of my staffers, Nadia. - She's a prostitute.
jokes.setup: Confusion about congressman's companion
jokes.punchline: Ben's matter-of-fact clarification
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery revealing Washington's casual corruption

jokes.text: Maybe you ride the pine on this one for a period or two.
jokes.setup: Mike trying to protect Catherine from press attention
jokes.punchline: Another inappropriate hockey metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Mike's ongoing inappropriate sports metaphors

jokes.text: You noticed the hockey references? Shit, I thought they were seamless.
jokes.setup: Mike being called out on hockey references
jokes.punchline: His proud confession about trying to be subtle
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta moment revealing Mike's complete lack of subtlety awareness

jokes.text: You're the only woman who's ever threatened to grate my balls over a bowl of spaghetti.
jokes.setup: Buddy greeting Selina warmly
jokes.punchline: Specific violent threat as term of endearment
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific violent imagery used as nostalgic compliment

jokes.text: Our first responders were terribly traumatized by what happened to the first responders in New York.
jokes.setup: Explaining need for 9/11 memorial in Bozeman
jokes.punchline: Trauma from watching other people's trauma on TV
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of secondary trauma claims and memorial culture

jokes.text: The budget has a fatter, more womanly behind than myself.
jokes.setup: Roger asking Will to explain budget metaphor
jokes.punchline: Will's oddly specific self-comparison
jokes.explanation: Moderate humor from Will's uncomfortable body image metaphor

jokes.text: God, I feel like somebody's playing a trick on him. They're about to dump a bucket of pig's blood on his head.
jokes.setup: Watching Gary get romantic attention
jokes.punchline: Carrie reference about impending humiliation
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference expressing Amy's protective cynicism

jokes.text: I'm not saying you're gonna be in the top 20, but you would be a lock for, like, mid 40s.
jokes.setup: Dan trying to console Amy about the hot list
jokes.punchline: Backhanded encouragement about bottom-tier ranking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan cruelty - trying to help while making it worse

jokes.text: Look at him, he's smiling like his shit's all pine-fresh smelling.
jokes.setup: Watching Gary enjoy attention
jokes.punchline: Creative scatological metaphor for smugness
jokes.explanation: Colorful original metaphor perfectly capturing Gary's unusual confidence

jokes.text: Listen, I wish I was hip enough to hang here with you all, but I've got to go deal with... - You're a busy girl. - Tell Liz I love her.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to escape conversation with lesbian caucus member
jokes.punchline: Awkward assumption about Connie's relationship status
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Selina's stereotypical assumptions

jokes.text: I got it! I got it! The widow saw me filming her and she got mad. She called me some truly awful things. - Oh, thank you, Satan.
jokes.setup: Richard excitedly reporting his surveillance failure
jokes.punchline: Dan's sarcastic thanks to the devil for Richard's incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dan's exasperation with Richard's failures

jokes.text: I went to turn it on, but it was already on, so I turned it off. Then I went to turn it off, but it already was off, so I turned it on.
jokes.setup: Richard explaining why he missed filming
jokes.punchline: Circular logic of camera operation confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard moment - earnest explanation of complete logical failure

jokes.text: (SIGHS) I had such plans for you, Richard.
jokes.setup: Richard's camera explanation
jokes.punchline: Dan's disappointed paternal response
jokes.explanation: Good emotional beat showing Dan's genuine disappointment in Richard

jokes.text: Like Baxter.
jokes.setup: Discussion about congressman who could 'go either way' politically
jokes.punchline: Ben's deadpan reference to Baxter's bathroom incident implying sexual ambiguity
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - two words create perfect double entendre callback

jokes.text: Well, at least we know you don't need big balls. - Got large stones for sure.
jokes.setup: Congressman asking for Secretary of State position
jokes.punchline: Exchange about courage using ball/stones euphemisms
jokes.explanation: Basic wordplay on courage metaphors but fairly predictable

jokes.text: I was warned that you would say some hurtful things.
jokes.setup: Jaeger announcing his abstention after Selina calls him a dumbass
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was coached to expect Selina's abuse
jokes.explanation: Good reveal showing political operatives coached him for Selina's predictable reaction

jokes.text: she's got your balls in her hand and she's working your shaft just the way you like it. And moments before you're about to come all over her stupid Slavic face, she says, 'Nyet, after much prayerful consideration, I have to abstain from the upcoming blowjob.' Are you Nadia in this situation?
jokes.setup: Selina trying to explain abstention consequences to Jaeger
jokes.punchline: Elaborate sexual metaphor involving his escort
jokes.explanation: Peak Veep moment - brilliantly constructed metaphor combining vulgarity, politics, and character callbacks

jokes.text: You're trying to get up the courage to ask me to dance. (LAUGHS) Guilty.
jokes.setup: Selina confronting Tom about his scheming
jokes.punchline: Pretending she's caught him wanting to dance instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection showing Selina's strategic thinking and Tom's quick adaptation

jokes.text: (WHISPERS) the Cocksucking Backstabber.
jokes.setup: Selina pretending to remember Korean restaurant name
jokes.punchline: Whispered profane assessment of Tom
jokes.explanation: Excellent fake-out setup leading to perfectly delivered insult

jokes.text: Oh, right, right. Um... (WHISPERS) the Cocksucking Backstabber.
jokes.setup: Selina asking about Korean restaurant
jokes.punchline: Her whispered 'restaurant name' revealing she knows about Tom's meeting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - casual restaurant conversation becomes devastating confrontation

jokes.text: How stupid are you, you motherfucking snake?
jokes.setup: Selina confronting Tom privately
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to maximum hostility
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment showing Selina dropping all pretense

jokes.text: What? Charlie Baird? What does Charlie Baird have to do with any of this? - I don't know. He's got nothing to do with it.
jokes.setup: Selina bringing up Charlie Baird accusation
jokes.punchline: Her immediate admission it's irrelevant
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Selina's scattered angry accusations

jokes.text: How stupid are you, you motherfucking snake?
jokes.setup: The confrontation beginning
jokes.punchline: Selina's direct opening with compound insult
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Selina's fury with direct attack

jokes.text: No straight man remembers a woman's shoes.
jokes.setup: Argument about Tom remembering her green shoes
jokes.punchline: Selina's confident assertion about male behavior
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with sexual tension subtext

jokes.text: Well, you got me. I'm gay, so...
jokes.setup: Selina's assertion about straight men
jokes.punchline: Tom's sarcastic fake revelation
jokes.explanation: Good deflection attempt showing Tom's wit under pressure

jokes.text: Well, you got me. I'm gay, so...
jokes.setup: Selina's accusation about remembering shoes
jokes.punchline: Tom's sarcastic deflection claiming to be gay
jokes.explanation: Good deflection attempt showing Tom's wit under pressure

jokes.text: All right, I wanted to fuck your brains out! - There you go. And now you're trying to fuck me tonight. - No, I am fucking you tonight.
jokes.setup: Selina forcing Tom to admit attraction
jokes.punchline: Tom's angry admission followed by escalating metaphors
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with sexual and political metaphors intertwining perfectly

jokes.text: Listen, I'm a high functioning alcoholic and you're a sloppy weekend drunk. Now get with it.
jokes.setup: Ben confronting Mike about his performance
jokes.punchline: Professional distinction between types of drinking problems
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Ben's professional pride about his alcoholism

jokes.text: So why does your shirt say Jon H Ryan? - Fuck.
jokes.setup: Jonah introducing himself at bowling alley
jokes.punchline: Shirt has wrong name spelling
jokes.explanation: Good campaign incompetence gag showing attention to detail failures

jokes.text: Although pocket pool is more my game.
jokes.setup: Teddy saying he's looking forward to bowling
jokes.punchline: Sexual euphemism instead
jokes.explanation: Predictable crude humor from antagonist character

jokes.text: People like you, you look like us, but there's a little piece missing. You're like a different species and one that I hope gets struck from the Earth because you're a bully and Jonah Ryan stands up to bullies.
jokes.setup: Jonah confronting his abuser Teddy
jokes.punchline: Dehumanizing speech that sounds fascistic
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment showing Jonah's victim mentality becoming victimizer rhetoric

jokes.text: Okay, you and Boo-Boo the ball-fondling bear here have exactly two seconds to get the fuck out.
jokes.setup: Dan defending Jonah from harassment
jokes.punchline: Creative insult combining children's character with sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant creative insult mixing innocent and adult references

jokes.text: The teenager selling cocaine in the bathroom thought so, too.
jokes.setup: Sarcastic comment about bowling alley being great venue
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of drug dealer's opinion
jokes.explanation: Good throwaway line revealing venue's seediness

jokes.text: It's where Woodrow Wilson had some of his most significant strokes.
jokes.setup: Selina describing Green Room as historic
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to presidential medical emergencies
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor mixing presidential history with medical trauma

jokes.text: You're playing a very dangerous game of chicken with the head fucking hen.
jokes.setup: Selina threatening Congresswoman Nickerson
jokes.punchline: Creative barnyard metaphor for power dynamics
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor mixing farmyard hierarchy with political power and profanity

jokes.text: And then I'm gonna have the IRS crawl so far up your husband's colon, he's gonna wish the only thing they find is more cancer.
jokes.setup: Selina threatening Nickerson with various consequences
jokes.punchline: Extremely dark threat involving cancer
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that callbacks to earlier cancer mention - peak Selina cruelty

jokes.text: Or do I need to shove a box of White House M&Ms up your stretched-out, six-baby vag?
jokes.setup: Selina's continued threats to Nickerson
jokes.punchline: Crude reference to her many pregnancies
jokes.explanation: Brutal callback to earlier motherhood discussion, peak campaign desperation

jokes.text: You're not hot. Gary Welsh at State is. You're a typo.
jokes.setup: Amy revealing truth to Gary about hot list mix-up
jokes.punchline: Brutal reduction of his identity to a clerical error
jokes.explanation: Perfectly brutal delivery - Amy's cruelty distilled to pure essence

jokes.text: Fuck me, Amadeus.
jokes.setup: Seeing the disastrous news footage
jokes.punchline: Dan's highbrow classical reference as expletive
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Dan's pretentious cultural references even in crisis

jokes.text: Fuck me, Amadeus.
jokes.setup: Watching Jonah's disastrous video on news
jokes.punchline: Classical music reference as profane exclamation
jokes.explanation: Creative highbrow profanity showing Dan's cultural pretensions

jokes.text: After the national elections, the president may have found herself with a bad lie in the rough, but after stabilizing the economy, the House vote is starting to look like a gimme putt.
jokes.setup: Mike giving press briefing
jokes.punchline: Complete commitment to golf metaphors now
jokes.explanation: Good evolution of Mike's sports metaphor obsession from hockey to golf

tmdb_overview: At the White House Christmas party, Selina bargains for votes in hopes of breaking the election tie. Meanwhile, the staff is preoccupied with the annual '50 Hottest D.C. Staffers' list; Mike eyes a job outside politics; and Dan helps Jonah with debate prep in New Hampshire.
insight: Cringe escalation sustains 79 jokes across a single congressional event with relentless character chaos.
